phase1.explanation:
The super keyword is used to access parent class members:

1. super() — call parent constructor
   • Must be the FIRST statement in child constructor
   • If not called, Java automatically calls super() (no-arg constructor)

2. super.method() — call parent method
   • Used when overriding — extends parent behavior
   • Can call parent's version even if you overrode it

3. super.field — access parent field
   • If child has same field name

Constructor Chaining:
• Child constructor calls parent constructor
• Parent constructor calls its parent (Object class)
• Chain continues until Object

⚠️ If parent doesn't have a no-arg constructor, you MUST call super() with parameters!

phase2.errorMessage:
Test.java:14: error: constructor Person in class Person cannot be applied to given types;
    Student(String name, int age, String studentId) {
    ^
  required: String,int
  found: no arguments
  reason: actual and formal argument lists differ in length
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Person doesn't have a no-argument constructor, but the Student constructor doesn't call super() with parameters.

Java tries to call super() (no args) automatically, but it doesn't exist!

The fix: super(name, age); must be the first line in the child constructor.

Remember: If parent has no no-arg constructor, you MUST explicitly call super() with the right parameters.

phase3.prompt:
Create Employee class with:
• Fields: name (String), salary (double)
• Constructor
• Method: work() — prints "[name] is working"

Create Manager subclass with:
• Field: department (String)
• Constructor with super()
• Override work() — prints "[name] is managing the [department] department"
• Also call super.work() in the override

In main, create a Manager and call work().
dialogueHints: Constructor: Manager(String name, double salary, String department) { super(name, salary); this.department = department; }
Override work(): call super.work() then add new behavior
Use super() to call parent constructor
phase3.openingDialogue: Super is your connection to the parent class! 🔗
